title: Skill Integration
subtitle: Skill Integration and Management
description: In AI Agent systems, skills are atomic capability units requiring unified integration, registration, invocation, and lifecycle management mechanisms. This domain addresses how agents can flexibly discover, load, execute, and unload various skills while ensuring skill isolation and composability.
sub_problems: Skill registration and discovery: How to establish a unified skill registry supporting dynamic registration, version management, and metadata description
Skill loading and unloading: How to safely load and unload skills while preventing resource leaks and dependency conflicts
Skill invocation interface: How to define a unified skill invocation protocol supporting synchronous/asynchronous and streaming/non-streaming execution
Skill dependency management: How to handle inter-skill dependencies, version compatibility, and conflict resolution
Skill permissions and isolation: How to enforce permission control, resource limits, and execution isolation for skills
best_practices: Use interfaces/protocols to define skill specifications, ensuring pluggability and interoperability
Implement a skill metadata system (name, version, input parameters, output parameters, dependencies, etc.) supporting automatic discovery and documentation generation
Adopt factory pattern or dependency injection for skill lifecycle management, avoiding hardcoded coupling
Provide timeout control, error recovery, and logging mechanisms for skill execution
Design a skill composition framework supporting chained skill invocation and conditional branching execution
